mixpower 1.2.0
  • Reference
  • Articles
  • Changelog

Plug a custom data-generating function into the mixpower pipeline

Source: R/custom-dgp.R
mp_custom_dgp.Rd

Wraps a user-supplied data-generating function as a validated simulate_fun, so any design the built-in grammar does not cover — partially nested designs, multi-arm factors, nonlinear time trends, correlated predictors, or anything else — can still flow through mp_power(), mp_calibrate(), mp_compare_models(), and the reporting tools. The returned function is the escape hatch that keeps the core engine lean while leaving the design grammar open-ended.

Usage

mp_custom_dgp(fun, validate = TRUE)

Arguments

fun

A function function(scenario, seed = NULL) returning a data.frame.

validate

Validate the output each call (default TRUE): a data frame that contains all variables in scenario$formula.

Value

A function suitable as a scenario simulate_fun (see mp_scenario() and the simulate argument of mp_scenario_lme4()).

Details

The user function receives the scenario (and optionally seed) and must return a data.frame containing every variable in the scenario's model formula (the response and predictors/grouping factors). To keep mp_calibrate() / mp_compare_models() meaningful, read the focal effect from scenario$assumptions$fixed_effects rather than hard-coding it, so the null (effect = 0) can be generated when those tools set it.

See also

mp_scenario_lme4(), mp_scenario().

Examples

# \donttest{
if (requireNamespace("lme4", quietly = TRUE)) {
  # A three-arm design (omnibus test) via a custom DGP.
  dgp <- function(scenario, seed = NULL) {
    n <- scenario$design$clusters$subject
    b <- scenario$assumptions$fixed_effects
    arm <- factor(rep(c("a", "b", "c"), length.out = n))
    mu <- c(a = 0, b = b$arm_b, c = b$arm_c)[as.character(arm)]
    data.frame(subject = factor(seq_len(n)), arm = arm,
               y = mu + stats::rnorm(n, sd = scenario$assumptions$residual_sd))
  }
  d <- mp_design(list(subject = 90), trials_per_cell = 1)
  a <- mp_assumptions(list("(Intercept)" = 0, arm_b = 0.4, arm_c = 0.7),
                      residual_sd = 1)
  scn <- mp_scenario_lme4(y ~ arm + (1 | subject), design = d, assumptions = a,
                          simulate = dgp, test_method = "lrt",
                          null_formula = y ~ 1 + (1 | subject))
  mp_power(scn, nsim = 20, seed = 1)
}
#> <mp_power>
#>   nsim: 20
#>   alpha: 0.05
#>   failure_policy: count_as_nondetect
#>   power: 0.000
#>   mcse: 0.000
#>   95% CI (clopper-pearson): [0.000, 0.168]
#>   diagnostics:
#>     - fail_rate: 1.000
#>     - singular_rate: 0.000
#>     - type_s: NA
#>     - type_m: NA
# }
